schema.idea: Seismic Activity Regional Construction Halt Shock

data.source: USGS earthquake activity (magnitude >4.5) via earthquake_activity adapter

schema.behavior: Magnitude 5.0+ earthquake in a major metro area triggers 2–4 week construction permit freeze, reducing materials demand and industrial activity.

market_impact.rationale: Materials demand drops when construction halts for safety assessments and permit reviews.

trade_logic.entry: If magnitude 5.0+ quake occurs in CA, TX, or NY region, short XLB
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 14 trading days or if construction activity resumes (permits reissued)

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Moderate earthquakes occur 1–2 times per month in active regions; magnitude 5.0+ hits major metros 3–4 times per year on average.
